name: Debbie Wasserman Schultz
subject: gator skin tough
disclaimer_text: Paid for and Authorized by Debbie Wasserman Schultz for Congress
body:
|
---
| | I like to say that over the years, I've developed "gator skin." It happens when you have to run your first race for office against five opponents with more money and more connections than you have. It happens when you spend years being the most outspoken critic of Republican hypocrisy, and calling out leaders like Paul Ryan and President Trump for their failures. You can always count on me to continue to speak out because it is right and because I have you by my side. I am so fortunate to have a network of amazing supporters who take action on the issues that matter and have stuck with me -- yes, I mean you all. And today, I'm asking you to make the choice to take action once again. | | | [Donate Now](https://secure.debbiewassermanschultz.com/page/m/10113cc1/622b7b5b/1e3f80bb/307b7eda/4048316231/VEsH/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/dwse181129pm?refcode=dwse181129pm_m&recurring=true&amount=5")
---
[**Tomorrow is our deadline to reach our first finance goal since Election Day -- and we're still about $2,200 short. Will you be one of the people to step up and give before November ends?**](https://secure.debbiewassermanschultz.com/page/m/10113cc1/622b7b5b/1e3f80bb/307b7eda/4048316231/VEsE/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/dwse181129pm?refcode=dwse181129pm_m&recurring=true&amount=5")
_If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately:_

**[Or donate another amount.](https://secure.debbiewassermanschultz.com/page/m/10113cc1/622b7b5b/1e3f80bb/307b7edc/4048316231/VEsC/p/eyJKU1ZEVDA1VEpTVT0iOiI4NjE3NjkiLCJlM3R6YkhWbmZYMD0iOiJkd3NlMTgxMTI5cG0iLCJlM3QyWlhKemFXOXVmWDA9IjoibSJ9/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/dwse181129pm?refcode=dwse181129pm_m_other&refcode2=861769")**
I'm sorry, I know you probably have a lot of email to get through right now. [**But I assure you, every single contribution matters.**](https://secure.debbiewassermanschultz.com/page/m/10113cc1/622b7b5b/1e3f80bb/307b7eda/4048316231/VEsD/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/dwse181129pm?refcode=dwse181129pm_m&recurring=true&amount=5")
This is how we build a network as tough as gator skin -- little by little, dollar by dollar, year over year. I can't tell you how much I appreciate each and every expression of generosity and support.
Thank you.
Debbie

name: Aisling Kerins, Team Inslee
subject: Jay has worked so hard
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Jay Inslee for Washington
body:
We can't fight this fight without you.

| [](https://action.jayinslee.com/page/m/7bd7b399/2f5ea88d/7b1cf720/2cbdaeeb/1594707150/VEsH/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/inslee-freeze?utm_source=ema_2018-11-29_eom-2&refcode=ema_2018-11-29_eom-2&utm_campaign=eom-2&utm_medium=ema") Friend, I've watched this past year as Jay worked harder than I've ever seen him work:
* To fight for Democratic candidates in Washington state and in gubernatorial races across the country.
* To ensure that Washington state was leading the charge in combating climate change.
* To combat voter suppression and inspire one of the best turnouts for a midterm election in our country's history.

And you know what, I've never seen him more energized or his work more impactful. Indeed, we saw huge wins time and time again. Washington state is demanding true progressive leadership and America is yearning to get our country back on track. But here's the rub -- **we won't get there unless you step up -- right now.** [**Donate $5 or more and help us reach our end-of-month goal of $20,000 right now -- our last monthly deadline before our state-mandated fundraising freeze.**](https://action.jayinslee.com/page/m/7bd7b399/2f5ea88d/7b1cf720/2cbdaeeb/1594707150/VEsE/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/inslee-freeze?utm_source=ema_2018-11-29_eom-2&refcode=ema_2018-11-29_eom-2&utm_campaign=eom-2&utm_medium=ema") Even though we're making big moves forward, there is still _so much_ to be done. Donald Trump is still spewing his hateful, racist politics and too many Republicans are still following him like sheep. And, every day, the reality and impact of climate change is looking more and more dire. But that's not all -- in less than three weeks, we are going to be facing our fundraising freeze, meaning we won't be able to accept contributions for the whole time the legislature is in session. There's no time to wait. We need your help now. **Please, help us reach our goal today:[https://secure.actblue.com/donate/inslee-freeze](https://action.jayinslee.com/page/m/7bd7b399/2f5ea88d/7b1cf720/2cbdaeeb/1594707150/VEsF/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/inslee-freeze?utm_source=ema_2018-11-29_eom-2&refcode=ema_2018-11-29_eom-2&utm_campaign=eom-2&utm_medium=ema")** Onward and upward. Thank you, Aisling Kerins | [CONTRIBUTE »](https://action.jayinslee.com/page/m/7bd7b399/2f5ea88d/7b1cf720/2cbdaeeb/1594707150/VEsC/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/inslee-freeze?utm_source=ema_2018-11-29_eom-2&refcode=ema_2018-11-29_eom-2&utm_campaign=eom-2&utm_medium=ema")
---

_Now more than ever, we must support progressive leadership in America. Chip in to show you're with Jay Inslee as we fight for progress in Washington state and around the country._

| [CONTRIBUTE »](https://action.jayinslee.com/page/m/7bd7b399/2f5ea88d/7b1cf720/2cbdaeeb/1594707150/VEsD/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/inslee-freeze?utm_source=ema_2018-11-29_eom-2&refcode=ema_2018-11-29_eom-2&utm_campaign=eom-2&utm_medium=ema")

subject: Organizing is a long-term effort
disclaimer_text: Paid for and Authorized by Debbie Wasserman Schultz for Congress
body:
|
---
| | It might seem odd to you that this close after Election Day, we have another goal to reach. But organizing isn't a short-term project -- it's a long-haul effort. We ask you to [**chip in and help us hit our $8,000 November goal**](https://secure.debbiewassermanschultz.com/page/m/10113cc6/622b7b5b/1e35ca73/307b7ec5/38566577/VEsH/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/dwse181130am?refcode=dwse181130am_m&recurring=true&amount=5") now so that next month, we're in a position to hit our final FEC goal of the year. name: Jason Yates
subject: It’s a matter of life and death.
body:
| | President Trump: Grant Asylum to Asia Bibi
---
[ ](https://www2.myfaithvotes.org/r?u=HTlSzGOWwx5Ys3a30u92SccQJDgjudQo1EknvTst9mg&e=be53806c85fc17bd685e1e4c4d896e92&utm_source=myfaithvotes&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=e181130p2a01&n=1)
| Peter, It’s been almost a month since Asia Bibi was found “not guilty” of Pakistan’s blasphemy law and released from prison. But her life is in just as much danger now as when she faced the death penalty because of her Christian faith. Ever since Pakistan’s Supreme Court acquitted her of blasphemy charges on October 31st, Pakistani Islamic leaders have actively sought to kill her, her family, and the three judges who ruled in her favor. It’s now being reported that the Islamic hardliners are hunting her down, going house to house in Pakistan searching for her. Bibi is being kept in protective custody by the Pakistani Government for her own safety. However, the government has said that they will not stand in the way of the Islamic hardliners trying to start another review process of her case. If this happens, she could be prohibited from leaving the country – a country ranked 5th in the world for extreme religious persecution. Being forced to stay in the country would mean almost certain death. We must pray this does not happen. Asia Bibi’s husband has pleaded for asylum in the UK (which the UK has denied), Canada and the United States, but so far the United States has not issued asylum to Asia Bibi. **It’s time for us to speak up.** President Trump and his administration have been strong advocates for religious freedom across the globe, but they must act now and offer asylum to Asia Bibi and her family. **Please do two things:**
1. Send an email and/or tweet to President Trump, your representatives in Congress, and Secretary Pompeo [using our simple tool and pre-written message](https://www2.myfaithvotes.org/r?u=IZ_jVElWb7Dgj8GT1ObVEhfmU8jCsquOllfvvhYvaZc&e=be53806c85fc17bd685e1e4c4d896e92&utm_source=myfaithvotes&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=e181130p2a01&n=2) and urge them to offer Asia Bibi and her family asylum
2. Pray daily for Asia Bibi’s safety and that Pakistan will not block her from leaving the country

[](https://www2.myfaithvotes.org/r?u=IZ_jVElWb7Dgj8GT1ObVEhfmU8jCsquOllfvvhYvaZc&e=be53806c85fc17bd685e1e4c4d896e92&utm_source=myfaithvotes&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=e181130p2a01&n=3) Share this message with as many Christians as you can who will stand with us in prayer and action to see Asia Bibi and her family protected. **United. We Stand.** Jason Yates
CEO
[](https://www2.myfaithvotes.org/r?u=FvZODUdzRCV4_QVEoUI5Y_LYDGbWPUdf00keax2EMv8&e=be53806c85fc17bd685e1e4c4d896e92&utm_source=myfaithvotes&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=e181130p2a01&n=4) P.S. If you are on social media, after you send your message, make sure to share the page with others using the Facebook and Twitter links on the bottom of the page to send a strong and unified message to our leaders!
---

>
> In case you missed it, the Trump administration released the Fourth National Climate Assessment report last Friday, and the results are far worse than we could have anticipated.

>
> _**[We need your help. The Trump administration is refusing to accept the conclusions of its own report, and we need to prove that there’s public support on our side. Add your name if you agree that the government should be doing more to combat climate change →](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/3444382/24543687/-1719668151?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDUvMS83MDg4MCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJlNzI3ZTc1NC1kNGY0LWU4MTEtOWYyYS0yODE4Nzg0ZjliNjUiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=i82q4JVKtQaSgvFf1SazWzp41uQbQ8qdXhytdNu7oIs=)**_

>
> _[**Protecting our environment and combating climate change should not be a partisan issue. We need your name to prove that the American people expect more action. Sign now →**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/3444383/24543688/-1719668151?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDUvMS83MDg4MCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJlNzI3ZTc1NC1kNGY0LWU4MTEtOWYyYS0yODE4Nzg0ZjliNjUiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=i82q4JVKtQaSgvFf1SazWzp41uQbQ8qdXhytdNu7oIs=)_

Friends,
Welcome to the team! Let me introduce myself: I'm Catherine, and I'm proud to represent Nevada in the U.S. Senate.
After a close, hard-fought campaign, grassroots supporters like you helped us win this critical Senate seat for Democrats. And on election night, I promised to be one hell of a check on Donald Trump.
I'm doing everything I can to defend our progress from Trump and the GOP, stand up to Big Oil and the Big Banks and fight for hardworking families - but with so much of what we care about at stake, I need you on my side.

subject: President Trump Opens Foreign Trip with a Historic Trade Deal
body:
Earlier this morning, President Donald J. Trump signed a three-way trade deal with Mexico and Canada... | | | | [ ](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=1d1ede3992&e=3ee4985eea)
---
| | |
# **President Trump Signs NAFTA Replacement Deal with Mexico, Canada**

Earlier this morning, President Donald J. Trump signed a three-way trade deal with Mexico and Canada that will replace the outdated NAFTA if it is approved by Congress. “Trump signed the U.S. Mexico Canada trade agreement in Argentina on the side of the G-20 summit,” Pete Kasperowicz reports in the _Washington Examiner_.

“This is a model agreement that changes the trade landscape forever, and this is an agreement that first and foremost benefits working people, something of great importance to all three of us here today,” the President said.

[Click here to read more.](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=840755d07a&e=3ee4985eea)
---
|
---
| “The International Labor Organization estimates that worldwide, nearly 25 million children and adults of all ages and backgrounds are victims of human trafficking, including forced labor and sex trafficking. Every government in the world has a moral obligation to do all in its power to stop these heinous crimes within its borders,” Advisor to the President Ivanka Trump writes in _[The Washington Post](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=99cae8b880&e=3ee4985eea)_. “That is why President Trump took strong action on Thursday to hold accountable those governments that have persistently failed to meet the minimum standards for combating human trafficking in their countries.”
---
|
---
| “Son-in-law and senior adviser to the president Jared Kushner received Mexico’s highest honor awarded to foreigners on Friday. Mexican President Enrique Pena Nieto bestowed the Mexican Order of the Aztec Eagle to Kushner in a ceremony that President Trump made an unscheduled stop at Friday morning,” Katelyn Caralle reports in the _[Washington Examiner](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=fbdc5612d5&e=3ee4985eea)_. “I believe we are at a historic place in the relationship between our two countries,” Mr. Kushner said during the ceremony.
---
|
---
| “U.S. Border Patrol agents have arrested a member of the infamous Salvadoran MS-13 gang who admitted to authorities that he traveled with a caravan of Central American migrants who were hoping to qualify for asylum in America,” Mairead McArdle reports for _[National Review](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=f3abe446c7&e=3ee4985eea)_. “During questioning at the El Centro station, the Honduran citizen confessed that he is an active member of MS-13 and had intended to enter the country illegally after traveling to the U.S. with the caravan of thousands of other migrants. He is in custody pending his deportation back to Honduras.”
---
|
---
| “New numbers from the Department of Homeland Security show that just 9 percent of asylum claims made by individuals from Central America turn out to be legitimate,” Katie Pavlich reports for _[Townhall](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=da58f4aa11&e=3ee4985eea)_. “The fact that only 9 percent of those who initially claim asylum are found eligible, indicates that we are expending most of our limited resources – detention space, court space and the time of our asylum officers and immigration judges – denying frivolous or illegitimate claims of asylum from the 9 out of 10 who are found ineligible,” DHS said in a statement.
---
|
---
|

---

He is in custody pending his deportation back to Honduras.” | | “New numbers from the Department of Homeland Security show that just 9 percent of asylum claims made by individuals from Central America turn out to be legitimate,” Katie Pavlich reports for . “The fact that only 9 percent of those who initially claim asylum are found eligible, indicates that we are expending most of our limited resources – detention space, court space and the time of our asylum officers and immigration judges – denying frivolous or illegitimate claims of asylum from the 9 out of 10 who are found ineligible,” DHS said in a statement. | | | | | | | The White House 1600 Pennsylvania Ave NW Washington, DC 20500 * USA · 202-456-1111

| | \---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- Derek:

I've emailed you about a number of important recount efforts and runoff elections over the past few weeks, and there's one more you should know about: [**This Tuesday, December 4th, Democratic nominee for Georgia Secretary of State John Barrow is in a critical runoff.**](https://secure.timkaine.com/page/m/330f1758/3e0906/7a3ecd99/3e374620/2417653750/VEsH/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/tk-barrow?amount=35&refcode=20181130_1_FR_TK_Barrow&utm_medium=email&utm_source=kaine&utm_content=1+-+This+Tuesday+December+4th+Democratic+nom&utm_campaign=20181130_1_FR_TK_Barrow&source=20181130_1_FR_TK_Barrow")

name: Bernie Sanders
subject: The White House has threatened to veto our Yemen bill. So here’s what we have to do.
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Friends of Bernie Sanders
body:
It is not the president who gets to decide whether or not we go to war in Yemen, or Syria, or anywhere else. It is Congress.
#

Sisters and Brothers -
Earlier this week, the United States Senate took the first step toward ending our country’s active participation in a morally indefensible war in Yemen — a war most Americans know little about and that Congress never voted to authorize.
Over the last several years, our support for the Saudi-led war in Yemen has been one of the great moral stains on our nation resulting in the worst humanitarian crisis on earth. During the last several years more than 85,000 children in Yemen have starved to death, while millions more of the people there face imminent starvation. With the water infrastructure destroyed by Saudi bombs, 10,000 new cases of cholera occur each week as people are unable to find clean drinking water.
And what my bill, offered with Senators Mike Lee of Utah and Chris Murphy of Connecticut, says is that instead of being part of the killing in Yemen, on behalf of a despotic Saudi regime, we should be doing everything possible to create a peaceful resolution to that war and provide the kind of humanitarian help Yemenis so desperately need.
With 63 votes in favor and 37 against, this is the first time in the history of the United States that the U.S. Senate has voted to advance a resolution withdrawing the United States Armed Forces from an unauthorized and unconstitutional war.
And our success has a lot to do with people at the grassroots level making their voices heard on this important issue.
But we still have work left to do.
Next week, I expect that we have another procedural vote and then a final vote on passage of the resolution. So we cannot stop now. Not if we want to see this victory through to the end and limit our engagement in Yemen to the humanitarian and diplomatic aid needed to relieve the suffering of millions of Yemenis. Add your name if you’re with me:
**[Tell the U.S. Senate: It’s time to end the United States’ support for the Saudi-led war in Yemen. We must make it crystal clear the American people want our country out of this horrible civil war.](https://act.berniesanders.com/signup/yemen_petition/?source=em181130-full&t=1&akid=379%2E150750%2EkD2PsX)**
For decades and under presidents of both parties, Republicans and Democrats have abdicated their Constitutional responsibility to debate and decide whether or not our country engages in war.
It is not the president who gets to decide whether or not we go to war in Yemen, or Syria, or anywhere else. It is Congress.
And our vote earlier this week was a big step forward in reasserting that constitutional responsibility.
Not surprisingly, given our president's deep affection for authoritarian regimes, the White House has threatened to veto this bill if it gets to Trump’s desk. So what I want to do today with this petition is make sure Trump knows that it isn’t just the United States Senate that believes we should end our involvement in this humanitarian catastrophe, but the American people as well.
So add your name:
**[Tell the U.S. Senate: It's time to end the United States’ support for the Saudi-led war in Yemen.](https://act.berniesanders.com/signup/yemen_petition/?source=em181130-full&t=2&akid=379%2E150750%2EkD2PsX)**
Enough is enough. Enough killing. Enough starvation. Enough destruction. This is the time to tell Saudi Arabia, and indeed the rest of the world, that we will no longer be a partner to the horrific crisis in Yemen.
In solidarity,
Bernie Sanders
* * *

**Tell the U.S. Senate: It's time to end the United States’ support for the Saudi-led war in Yemen.**

To support Bernie with a contribution, [click here](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/bernie-em-footer?refcode=em-footer&t=5&akid=379%2E150750%2EkD2PsX).

subject: Building our team
body:
Here in Indiana, it seems like we’re so lucky. We’ve got a great team of Republican leaders who have put Indiana on the right track. Whether you're... | | | [View this email in your browser](https://mailchi.mp/indiana/building-our-team?e=1acd9bb3d6)
---
| |
---
|
---
| **_Building Our Team_** Here in Indiana, it seems like we’re so lucky. We’ve got a great team of Republican leaders who have put Indiana on the right track. Whether you’re at the Statehouse and watching folks like **Governor Eric Holcomb** at work, or you’re visiting one of Indiana’s overwhelmingly Republican-led county courthouses, you’ll see leaders delivering results.

But these results don’t just happen. And it’s not really luck. It takes years of building up a team, and that team building is happening right now – even as the next election is still months away.

Take next year’s municipal elections, for instance. The work led by our Republican mayors, councils and our other local leaders in cities and towns across Indiana plays an important role in critical issues like infrastructure, economic growth and quality of life. They’re important members of the team – and if they do good work, they’re prime candidates for promotions to countywide or even potentially statewide positions.

But building our team doesn’t stop there. Look at county party executive teams, our grassroots volunteers and even our state party interns (we’re currently recruiting – so [send your favorite college student our way](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=9c3c6bc9f1&e=1acd9bb3d6)), and you’ll find the next generation of Republicans who will continue the tradition of delivering great government service.

We’re building our team. And we need great Republicans like you to be a part of it. -Kyle Hupfer

_**PHOTO:** Chairman Kyle Hupfer addresses a sold-out crowd at this year's Congress of Counties training event in January, which helped us build the team that supported our 2018 -- and future -- Republican victories. _
---
|
---
| | | **GOVERNOR HOLCOMB'S WEEK THAT WAS**
---
| [ ](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=8ff15db3dd&e=1acd9bb3d6)
---
| **_A Fighting Spirit & A Big Honor_** This week, **Governor Eric Holcomb** presented a big award and accepted a big new responsibility. First, he had the opportunity to present Purdue superfan and cancer fighter Tyler Trent with Indiana’s highest civilian honor, the Sagamore of the Wabash.

Tyler is a 20-year-old Purdue student who is fighting bone cancer and using his diagnosis as a force for good. [Read more about Tyler](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=4d29e21e5c&e=1acd9bb3d6), his positivity, his love of rooting for Purdue football, his work supporting cancer research, and some [other big honors](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=4481e426a7&e=1acd9bb3d6) that are also coming his way.

And then yesterday, **Governor Holcomb** was elected as the policy chairman of the Republican Governors Association (RGA) -- an organization that works to elect Republican governors across the country. As policy chairman of the RGA,**Governor Holcomb** will be responsible for elevating the proven policies that Republican governors are bringing to Americans in states across the country -- including right here in Indiana. Way to go, governor!
---
|
---
| [ ](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=beda6c7841&e=1acd9bb3d6)
---
| **_Now Hiring: Intern for the Indiana GOP!_** Looking for an internship? Know a college student who loves politics?

The Indiana Republican Party is currently [accepting resumes](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=33369b5db7&e=1acd9bb3d6) for students interested in an internship during the Spring or Summer 2019 semesters! With paid opportunities available, we’re looking for college students interested in gaining professional experience or getting their start in politics.

Visit [Indiana.gop/internships](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=28803c5205&e=1acd9bb3d6) to apply for internships in our political, finance and communications departments, and feel free to reach out to Derek Hugo, Indiana GOP internship coordinator, at [dhugo@indiana.gop](mailto:dhugo@indiana.gop) with any questions.
---
|
---
| **_Upcoming Republican Events_** [December 1](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=4962bda874&e=1acd9bb3d6): Kosciusko County Republican Women's Christmas Party
[December 3](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=31315eb2af&e=1acd9bb3d6): Greater Indianapolis Republican Women's Club Annual Holiday Luncheon
[December 6](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=8b52b6e3f1&e=1acd9bb3d6): Porter County Annual GOP Christmas Party
[December 7](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=503b063046&e=1acd9bb3d6): 9th District Christmas Party
[December 8](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=c76de55aab&e=1acd9bb3d6): Wells County Republican Christmas Open House
[April 6](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=7add17392b&e=1acd9bb3d6): Decatur County Lincoln Day Dinner
[April 13](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=4dda450f73&e=1acd9bb3d6): Vermillion County Lincoln Day Dinner

disclaimer_text: Paid for and Authorized by Debbie Wasserman Schultz for Congress
body:
|

| | Trump's fear-mongering rhetoric doesn't scare me. I've seen people march to defend the rights of their friends and neighbors. I've seen people of all backgrounds come together to affect change.
